rationale: Both evaluations land on the same 6/10 score and rest on the same substantive reasoning: strong polyglot full-stack breadth, Sociomantic high-throughput experience, Staff-level AI-agent/MCP work, with the same core gaps (Python missing as a must-have, limited evidence of distributed-systems/strategic leadership at staff scope, and unclear on-call/incident-response ownership). The only differences are the employer names echoed from the resume change (Stripe instead of re:cinq) and ordinary rewording. The famous-employer substitution did not buy the candidate any score lift, new strength, or removed concern, so the variation is within normal run-to-run noise.
